summary: dtxtools postinstall performs host reconnaissance and exfiltrates results to attacker OAST endpoint
details: On `npm install`, package.json declares a postinstall script that runs `find /` to locate database client binaries (mysql, mongo, mongosh, psql, redis-cli, sqlite3, elasticsearch), writes the inventory to /data/db_clients_check.txt, and then curl-POSTs file contents over plain HTTP to a Burp Collaborator subdomain at 3dhd6wwmusbh04m22igmzvb4hvnmblza.oastify.com. The package is advertised as a generic string-utility library (camelToKebab, slugify, etc.) with empty author, repository, homepage, and bugs metadata; the index.js exports function as cover for the install-time exfiltration. The harm fires automatically on default install without any caller action, and the advertised string-utility purpose does not justify filesystem reconnaissance or outbound POSTs.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
